semanticCore: Breaking without separating, cracking, splitting, or partial fracture
quranicRelevance: No breaking/severing for it (Infisam - 'la infisama laha').

keyDerivations.meaning: Cleaving, cracking, breaking, or severing without complete detachment

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوتِ وَيُؤْمِنْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ لَا انْفِصَامَ لَهَا (2:256): Then whoever disbelieves in false deities and believes in God has grasped the most trustworthy handhold with no breaking/cracking in it.
summaryEn: The root refers to a crack or split that breaks a structure's integrity (Fasm), describing the absolute durability of the covenant of faith as an unbreakable handhold.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التصدع والكسر من غير انفصال أو انقطاع تام للشيء (فصم)، مشبهاً الإيمان بالعروة الوثقى المحكمة التي لا يمكن أن تنفصم أو تنكسر أبداً.
rawText: أَفْصَمُ An anklet much cracked. (El-Hejeree, M, K. *) افصم المَطَرُ 4 The rain passed away, or ceased. (S, M, K.) And افصمت عَنْهُ الحُمَّى The fever quitted him: (S, K, * TA:) in the K, erroneously. افصم الحُمَّى. (TA.) And one says, دَآءٌ 🡻يَفْصِمُ وَلَا يُفْصَمُ A disease that breaks and does not pass away. (TA.) === And افصم is said of a stallion [camel], meaning He ceased, or abstained, from covering. (TA.) انفصم 7 It broke (S, M, Msb, K) without becoming separated: (S. M, Msb:) [i. e. it be came cracked:] and 🡻تفصّم has a similar meaning [i. e. it became cracked much, or in several places]. (S, M, K.) You say, انفصم ظَهْرُهُ His back cracked. (TA.) And انفصمت الدُّرَّةُ The pearl cracked in one part thereof. (TA.) === And It broke, or became severed, or cut off. (K.) It is said in the Kur [ii. 257], لَا ٱنْفِصَامَ لَهَا (S, M, Msb, TA) meaning There shall be no breaking incident to it. (M, TA.) === And It opened so as to form an interstice, or a gap. (TA in art. شظى.) تَفَصَّمَ 5 see the next paragraph. دُمْلُجٌ مَفْصُومٌ [A cracked armlet of silver: to this, thrown down and neglected, Dhu-r-Rummeh likens a sleeping gazelle]. (S, TA.) فصم السِّوَاكِ [app. فَصْم, originally an inf. n., but probably, I think, a mistranscription for قَصْم,] A fragment [of the stick with which the teeth are cleaned]. (TA.) فَأْسٌ فَصِيمْ A large [hoe, or adz, or the like]. (Fr, K.) فَصَمَهُ 1 , (S, M, Msb, K,) aor. ﹻ, (M, Msb, K,) inf. n. فَصْمٌ, (S, M, Msb,) He broke it (S, M, Msb, K) without separating: (S, M, Msb:) [i. e. he cracked it:] and 🡻فصّمهُ [he cracked it much, or in several places], (M, TA,) inf. n. تَفْصِيمٌ. (TA.) And فَصَمَ الخُرْزَةَ [He cracked, or tore without separating, the seam, or suture, of a skin] (K and TA in art. خرم: in the CK قَصَمَ and الخَرَزَةَ.) القَصْمُ, with ق, signifies “ the breaking so as to separate. (TA.) === See also 4. === فُصِمَ. said of a house, or tent, (K,) or of the side thereof, (M,) It became thrown down, or demolished. (M, K.) === And وَصْمٌ signifies A thing's being cracked. (A'Obeyd, TA.) فَصَّمَ 2 see the preceding paragraph. فَصْمَةٌ A crack in a wall. (TA.)
